task:
## GitHub Issue: always-further/nono#269

fix: audit trail not saved for sessions without writable tracked paths

## Summary

`nono audit list` shows no sessions for most runs. Session metadata is only written when both `--rollback` is active **and** there are user-specified directories with write access as discovered in https://github.com/always-further/nono/issues/268 . Read-only sessions, sessions without `--rollback`, and sessions using Monitor mode all produce no audit trail.

## Reproduction

```
nono run --rollback --allow-cwd -- echo hello
nono audit list --today
# [nono] No sessions found matching filters.
```

Even though `--rollback` is explicitly passed, `--allow-cwd` grants `[read]` access. The rollback system only tracks writable paths, so `tracked_paths` is empty and no session directory is created.

### Verified test matrix

| Test | Flags | Audit entry? |
|------|-------|-------------|
| 1 | `--rollback --allow-cwd` (read-only) | No |
| 2 | `--rollback --allow .` (read+write) | **Yes** |
| 3 | `--supervised --allow .` (no rollback) | No |
| 4 | `--allow .` (Monitor mode) | No |

Only case #2 produces an audit entry.

## Root cause

Two layers in `crates/nono-cli/src/main.rs`:

1. **Writable path gate** (~line 1061-1075): `tracked_paths` filters for `AccessMode::Write | AccessMode::ReadWrite` with `CapabilitySource::User`. When all paths are read-only, `tracked_paths` is empty and `rollback_state` is set to `None` — no session directory, no `session.json`.

2. **Rollback flag gate** (~line 1057): Session metadata is only saved inside `if flags.rollback && !flags.no_rollback`, and the actual write happens inside `if let Some(...) = rollback_state` (~line 1278). Sessions without `--rollback` (including all Monitor mode runs) skip metadata entirely.

## Expected behavior

Every sandboxed execution should record an audit session in `~/.nono/rollbacks/` regardless of whether rollback snapshots are active or writable paths exist. The audit trail (command, start/end time, exit code, network events) is valuable independent of rollback.

## Fix

This should be addressed as part of #265 (remove Monitor, make Supervised default). With Supervised as the default execution strategy, the `--rollback` and `--supervised` flags go away, which removes the flag gates entirely. The fix should decouple audit from rollback: always create a session directory and write `session.json` for every execution. Rollback snapshots remain conditional on writable tracked paths, but audit metadata is always persisted.

The same bug exists in the #265 worktree — session metadata save is still inside the `if let Some(...) = rollback_state` block (~line 1310).

Audit should be on by default with an opt-out flag (e.g. `--no-audit`), not gated behind rollback or writable path presence.

Closes #268
